trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Agreeing to disagree, life on other worlds
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 11/17/25

urls.page_title: New course encourages students to embrace disagreement
urls.page_summary: A new course being offered will give students the chance to consider some of the most polarizing issues in our world today.

Join us for a night of dancing, card games and more to celebrate Jane Austen's 250th birthday! A regency dance workshop will take place from 5:45 PM to 6:45 PM. Snacks and refreshments will be available.
This event is open to all undergraduates....
urls.page_title: Chemist Geoffrey Coates wins Benjamin Franklin Medal
urls.page_summary: Coates is being recognized “for transformative work at the interface of chemical catalyst design and polymer science.”
urls.page_title: Physicist John Reppy wins Buckley Prize
urls.page_summary: Reppy was recognized along with David Bishop, Ph.D. ’78, for "groundbreaking experiments" they did on helium 50 years ago.
urls.page_title: Share In/Share Out: End-of-Semester PMA Dance Showcase - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Join the Department of Performing and Media Arts for Share In/Share Out: End-of-Semester PMA Dance Showcase. The showcase will take place in the Class of ’56 Dance Studio Theatre, Schwartz Center for the Performing Arts, on Friday, November 21, at...
urls.page_title: A&S launches Cornell in Los Angeles semester study away
urls.page_summary: The program will place students in Hollywood for a semester of coursework, networking, and cultural immersion.
urls.page_title: Translation offers English readers passage to satirical ‘Island of Hermaphrodites’
urls.page_summary: The novel, published anonymously in 1605, is "a very funny critique of court life that resonates for anyone dealing with very hierarchical institutions in which the exercise of power is often inscrutable and seemingly random,” says professor Kathl...
urls.page_title: Nathan Seiberg explores frontiers of physics in 2025 Bethe Lecture
urls.page_summary: Seiberg, professor in the School of Natural Sciences at the Institute for Advanced Study, will explore string theory and other aspects of scientific progress.
urls.page_title: Stucky Ensemble-in-Residence Splinter Reeds Performance (CU Music) - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
Stucky Ensemble-in-Residence Splinter Reeds performs a concert of works inspired by birdsong, including the world premiere of Hugo Morales Murguía’s Migrations, and pieces by Sam Pluta, Carola Bauckholt, and Dai Fujikura.
photo credit: Lenny Gonz...
